top_issue: BLVR/valve placement is entirely unsupported. The note is a routine tracheostomy tube exchange with stomal granulation treated by silver nitrate, not bronchoscopic lung-volume reduction.
Right side, drained volume (1,050 mL), and specimen panel could be more granular.
The note clearly documents Montgomery T-tube insertion, but no airway-stent/T-tube placement object is present.
Station 3P is hallucinated from the specimen label '4L node (3p)'; only stations 4L and 7 were sampled by EBUS.
Therapeutic aspiration/31645 is not clearly required by the note; secretion clearance may be incidental toileting during a navigational case.
established_tracheostomy_route=true and CPT 31615 are unsupported; this note is percutaneous tracheostomy placement, not bronchoscopy through an established trach.
The registry treats this as BLVR, but the note is valve placement for postoperative bronchopleural fistula/persistent air leak, not lung-volume reduction.
Bleeding is promoted to a moderate complication although the note describes mild diffuse mucosal hemorrhage controlled with cold saline.
Station 7 is labeled malignant by ROSE in the JSON, but the note says station 7 ROSE showed atypical cells.
The note's main therapeutic work is mucus plug extraction and lavage, but therapeutic aspiration is not represented.
inspection_findings lose negation and read as if there were an endobronchial abnormality, though the note says none.
Separate diagnostic bronchoscopy coding is likely overcalled in a PDT note where bronchoscopy is guidance rather than a standalone diagnostic service.
Montgomery T-tube insertion is documented, but no airway-stent/T-tube placement object is present.
airway_stent.action_type='revision' is unsupported; the note documents new Y-stent placement.
Fibrinolytic plan is future care, not same-day treatment, and is appropriately not turned on.
airway_stent.action_type='revision' and CPT 31638 are unsupported for this note; it documents new Montgomery T-tube insertion.
The note documents re-expansion pulmonary edema, but no complication structure is surfaced.
Chest-tube coding is not cleanly aligned with the note's emergent tube thoracostomy language (the note itself points toward 32551-style work, not 32556).
inspection_findings lose negation and imply an endobronchial mass despite the note stating no discrete endobronchial mass.
Station 10R is marked malignant in the JSON, but the note only gives malignant ROSE for station 4R; 10R was hemorrhagic/contaminated.
A pneumothorax complication is created from 'post-procedure CXR ordered / monitor for PTX' language even though the note states no intraoperative complication.
Bleeding is promoted to a moderate formal complication even though the note describes expected grade I laceration and bleeding controlled with cold saline.
Pass attribution across 10L vs 4L is not completely clean given the note's own inconsistent follow-up-pass wording.
Station 11L is treated as sampled even though the note says it was not visualized.
31645 is not clearly supported; secretion suctioning is incidental in a major tumor-debulking case.
airway_stent.performed=true and CPT 31638 are unsupported; the note explicitly says covered stent was evaluated and deferred.
inspection_findings are slightly malformed because diagnosis text bleeds into the inspection narrative.
procedures_performed is empty even though the note clearly documents flexible bronchoscopy through the tracheostomy.
The note's primary therapeutic action is mucus-cast extraction, but only BAL is represented.
Separate bronchoscopy coding (31622) is not clearly defensible in a PDT note.
The note is primarily a mature-tract tracheostomy tube exchange, but the registry surfaces only bronchoscopy coding.
Only bronchoscopy is surfaced, despite a difficult immature-tract trach reinsertion/exchange being the main intervention.
BAL volume is corrupted (3 mL from epinephrine instillation rather than BAL volume).
Montgomery T-tube insertion is documented, but no airway-stent/T-tube placement object is present.
thermal_ablation/31641 are unsupported; the note describes routine T-tube exchange with cold-forceps trimming of small stomal granulation, not thermal ablation.
The complication is converted into severe bleeding/Nashville 4, but the documented complication is posterior tracheal wall laceration with only minimal ooze.
intubation/31500 is unsupported; the patient was already intubated and the note describes ETT withdrawal, not a separate emergency intubation.
airway_stent is coded as revision/31638, but the note documents new covered SEMS placement across a TEF.
Station 4L is falsely treated as sampled even though the note says 4L was not identified.
pleurodesis.performed=true and CPT 32650 are unsupported; the note explicitly says pleurodesis was deferred pending pathology.
31640 is not well supported; the note describes minimal granulation trimming rather than tumor excision.
Sedation remains labeled Moderate despite propofol infusion plus paralytic and the note's explicit documentation conflict.
CPT 31573 is entirely unsupported.
Sedation is labeled Moderate even though no additional procedural sedation was given beyond baseline ICU infusions.
airway_abnormalities include extrinsic compression despite the note stating no extrinsic compression.
Severe bleeding/Nashville 3 is unsupported; the note describes minimal self-limited post-biopsy bleeding and mild hemoptysis that resolved before discharge.
Separate 31622 coding is not clearly supported beyond bronchoscopic guidance of the PDT.
Montgomery T-tube placement is documented but no airway-stent/T-tube procedure is represented.
The stent event is flattened into 'revision' even though the note describes migrated-stent extraction, balloon dilation, and new stent placement.
intubation is unsupported; the note explicitly says no intubation required.
